Apollo 11 Moon Mission

# Apollo 11 Moon Mission

### One small step for man, a giant leap for mankind.

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Jul 20, 2021

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

Share

*One small step for man, a giant leap for mankind.*

**July 20, 1969-**Apollo 11, landed on the Sea of Tranquility, Neil Armstrong became the first human to set foot on the moon, followed by Buzz Aldrin, while the 3rd one Michael Collins was in the command module Columbia. Initially it was Jim Lovell, who was the Command Module Pilot, as Collins had a back problem. However Collins did recover back in time, and Lovell, became part of the back up crew.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!-CXH!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fdeaa5b84-d982-4077-a431-a57e079ee6a4_673x680.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!-CXH!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fdeaa5b84-d982-4077-a431-a57e079ee6a4_673x680.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ceLr!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F5424f123-a6f5-4f0c-954f-d0ef51287eb0_680x535.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ceLr!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F5424f123-a6f5-4f0c-954f-d0ef51287eb0_680x535.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The backup crew for Apollo 11 was

Jim Lovell- Led the ill fated Apollo 13 mission to the Moon, on which that movie was based.

William Anders- Took the iconic Earth Rise photograph

Fred Haise- Later became 6th person to land on moon.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!a94V!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0d6f8e11-3ab5-45e7-81e5-670da0c22a25_220x275.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!a94V!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0d6f8e11-3ab5-45e7-81e5-670da0c22a25_220x275.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Qh6n!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0cde535a-34cf-4031-9707-ad9bc94f283c_210x263.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Qh6n!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0cde535a-34cf-4031-9707-ad9bc94f283c_210x263.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!eJ0I!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe5bf26af-2fa7-4725-b319-25db94fe84b2_220x275.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!eJ0I!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe5bf26af-2fa7-4725-b319-25db94fe84b2_220x275.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The command module of Apollo 11, was called Columbia, name of the giant cannon which fires a spacecraft in Jules Verne's iconic novel From the Eart to the Moon. The Lunar module was called Eagle after the national bird of US, as also it's insignia.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!m7e1!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fbb0ae35f-5307-44f8-af13-b0adf5001ed3_288x360.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!m7e1!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fbb0ae35f-5307-44f8-af13-b0adf5001ed3_288x360.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ow3j!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffc31969e-75ec-4c36-b23a-4e52c16911dc_220x198.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ow3j!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffc31969e-75ec-4c36-b23a-4e52c16911dc_220x198.jpeg)

The insignia for Apollo 11 was designed by Michael Collins, representing a peaceful lunar landing. Neil Armstrong's personal kit carried a piece of wood from the Wright brothers 1903 airplane's left propeller and a diamond studded astronaut's pin.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!eO79!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F50aa9e66-330b-40d6-a901-85a34de8dcaa_680x344.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!eO79!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F50aa9e66-330b-40d6-a901-85a34de8dcaa_680x344.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

On July 16, 1969 the historic Apollo 11 mission took off from Kennedy Space Center, launched by Saturn V, around 9:30 AM local time.12 minutes later it entered Earth's orbit, and after one and a half orbits, the S-IVB 3rd stage engine pushed it on trajectory.

July 19, 1969- Apollo 11 enters the lunar orbit, Sea of Tranquility was selected as the landing site, as it was relatively flat and unlikely to present major challenges to the landing craft.

July 20, 1969- Lunar Module Eagle separate from Command Module Columbia, leaving Michael Collins alone aboard on it. 5 minutes into the descent there were couple of false alarms breaking out, however Mission Control Center, Houston, advised crew to go ahead.

The Eagle in lunar orbit after separating from Columbia,.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oTeg!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ff9ced510-a81d-4d49-a616-abe752007f01_220x198.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oTeg!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ff9ced510-a81d-4d49-a616-abe752007f01_220x198.jpeg)

Backup pilots Jim Lovell and Fred Haise, listening to the news of the descent.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!HfKq!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3d2d4889-4ca9-4d9e-b943-544bcd9978b1_220x148.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!HfKq!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3d2d4889-4ca9-4d9e-b943-544bcd9978b1_220x148.jpeg)

Finally on July 20, Apollo 11 touched down on the moon at the Sea of Tranquility. Aldrin was the one constantly giving navigation information to Armstrong.

And Charles Duke of Capcom acknowledged the landing with "We copy you down Eagle" Houston, Tranquility Base Here, The Eagle has Landed"

One of the most famous call signs in history, matter of fact this was made up by Neil Armstrong himself, different from the original one. "Roger, Twan— Tranquility, we copy you on the ground. You got a bunch of guys about to turn blue. We're breathing again. Thanks a lot."

July 21, 1969- Neil Armstrong, opened the hatch of the Eagle, though had some difficulties squeezing in and out through it. Around 0:56 UTC, July 21 he set foot on the surface of the moon. Around 600 million people on Earth had a rather blurred image of this.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WIyJ!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F96d80b4a-56f8-49ee-93d7-b23eefc7a133_220x180.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WIyJ!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F96d80b4a-56f8-49ee-93d7-b23eefc7a133_220x180.jpeg)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!278n!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7143868d-f380-47e4-833c-4851e8a65f36_360x355.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!278n!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7143868d-f380-47e4-833c-4851e8a65f36_360x355.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

This was the original slowscan TV camera shot of Armstrong stepping on to the moon, and the other is the more well known photograph.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ZjxB!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffcaf1df1-429c-45be-b18f-e5f7d9e19c9c_327x260.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ZjxB!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffcaf1df1-429c-45be-b18f-e5f7d9e19c9c_327x260.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

After he landed on the Moon, Armstrong uttered the by now famous iconic quote, collected the soil samples, and 12 minutes later, Buzz Aldrin joined him. His words on seeing the moon- "Magnificient Desolation" .The astronauts planted a special;y designed flag on the Moon's surface. And Nixon spoke to them through a telephone radio transmission, that he described later as the most historic phone call ever made from the White House.

Finally after exploring the Moon's surface for some time, Aldrin and Armstrong returned back to the Eagle, carrying the soil samples they had collected. At 17:54 UTC, Eagle lifted off to rejoin Collins who was aboard Columbia.

All you see is the three of us, but beneath the surface are thousands and thousands of others, and to all of those, I would like to say, "Thank you very much" Buzz Aldrin on July 23, just before the splashdown of Apollo 11 mission

On July 24, 1969, the astronauts, returned home aboard Columbia, just before dawn touching down at a spot in the Pacific just east of Wake Island in American Samoa. Divers bought out them out and they were taken to the USS Hornet by Helicopter 66.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!gJQs!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7d0766ff-16c4-4587-98f1-975f468c04ad_220x211.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!gJQs!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7d0766ff-16c4-4587-98f1-975f468c04ad_220x211.jpeg)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!_Y_1!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4a10498a-2765-4aa1-8b34-1888265611d1_360x360.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!_Y_1!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4a10498a-2765-4aa1-8b34-1888265611d1_360x360.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The astronauts spent 21 days in quarantine aboard USS Hornet. Nixon was there on the ship, to personally welcome them on their return. And finally on August 10, 1969, they came out of quarantine, and on August 13, were paraded in NYC, Chicago, LA.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!7rQE!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fc158526f-12ed-4b3f-b178-a5fbaf794af2_220x169.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!7rQE!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fc158526f-12ed-4b3f-b178-a5fbaf794af2_220x169.jpeg)
